title: Shirin Polo (Sweet Jeweled Rice)
description: A stunning, festive Persian sweet rice dish adorned with slivered almonds, pistachios, candied orange peel, and barberries, beautifully fragrant with saffron.

instructions.text: Wash the basmati rice and soak in salted water for 1 hour. Drain.

To remove bitterness from orange peel: Boil the julienned peels in water for 5 minutes, drain, and repeat this process twice. In a small pan, simmer the peels with 2 tbsp sugar and a little water until candied. Set aside.
In a skillet, sauté the carrots in 1 tbsp of butter until soft. Set aside.
In the same skillet, gently sauté the barberries, slivered almonds, and slivered pistachios with 1 tbsp butter and the remaining sugar for just 2 minutes. Do not burn.
Sauté the chopped onion until golden, then mix in the cooked chicken breast and a pinch of salt.
Parboil the soaked basmati rice in a large pot of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Drain.
In a pot, melt 2 tbsp butter. Layer the parboiled rice, mixing gently with the chicken, candied orange peels, carrots, and the nut/barberry mixture, reserving some nuts/peels for garnish.
Pour the bloomed saffron over the top. Cover tightly with a towel-wrapped lid and steam on low heat for 45 minutes. Serve garnished with the reserved nuts and peels.

tips: Take your time removing the bitterness from the orange peel; it makes all the difference. Buy pre-slivered pistachios to save prep time.
storage_notes: Store in the fridge for up to 4 days. Reheat gently in the microwave or stovetop.
serve_with: Extra chicken on the side if desired, or as a standalone dish for celebrations.

substitutions.note: Can easily be made vegetarian by skipping the meat altogether; the nuts and flavors hold their own.
Roughly chop dried cranberries if barberries are hard to find.
